issue_description: A truncated .zip-File produces an java.io.EOFException conatining a hughe amount of byte[]-data in the error-message - leading to beeps and crippeling workload in an potential console-logger.

org.apache.commons.compress.ArchiveUtils.error_msg:

org.apache.commons.compress.ArchiveUtils.clean_error_msg:
junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: expected:<...23456789012345678901[...]> but was:<...23456789012345678901[234567890123456789012345678901234567890123456789]>
	at org.apache.commons.compress.ArchiveUtilsTest.sanitizeShortensString(ArchiveUtilsTest.java:82)  assertEquals(expected, ArchiveUtils.sanitize(input));

buggy_code_comment:
/** * Returns a "sanitized" version of the string given as arguments, * where sanitized means non-printable characters have been * replaced with a question mark and the outcome is not longer * than 255 chars. * *

This method is used to clean up file names when they are * used in exception messages as they may end up in log files or * as console output and may have been read from a corrupted * input.

* * @param s the string to sanitize * @return a sanitized version of the argument * @since Compress 1.12 */
